summary: Verified Employee 'My Documents' onboarding upload/re-upload flow end-to-end. Backend pytest 7/7 passed (my-status returns 9 docs with correct state; verified doc blocked with 400 'already approved'; rejected pan_card re-upload succeeds and clears rejection_reason; not_uploaded photo upload succeeds; HR fan-out creates in-app notifications with link=/verification using 'uploaded'/'re-uploaded' verbs; dashboard/stats warm < 2s). Frontend UI verified: kasper sees 'My Documents' in sidebar (visible during 14-day bypass), Onboarding Documents section renders all 9 doc rows with correct status badges (Approved/green/Locked for Aadhaar, Rejected/red + reason + Re-upload for PAN, Not Uploaded + Upload for fresh docs, Pending Review + Replace for already-uploaded). Admin /verification page is unaffected and lists Kasper (EMP0050) 'In Progress' at the top.
critical_code_review_comments: Cold-start /api/auth/login is ~33s (warm ~0.4s) and cold /api/dashboard/stats is ~32s (warm 0.8-1.8s). Warm path meets the <2s SLO requested by this task, but the cold path is a concern for users hitting the app after idle. Consider a lightweight keep-warm ping or background warmup of the Mongo/aggregation pipelines.
server.py is 13,698 lines — the >700-line guideline is heavily violated; modular split (auth, onboarding, dashboard, payroll, attendance) would massively help maintainability.

Notification fan-out in upload_onboarding_document is fully wrapped in try/except (good — never fails the upload), but HR email send is hardcoded to a single address 'hr@blubridge.com'. If the org adds more HR mailboxes this becomes a stale config; consider reading from settings/env.

On the FE EmployeeDocuments page, the 'photo.jpg / voter_id.jpg' rows show generic Cloudinary file names because we POSTed synthetic data — this is expected for testing but in prod the file_name comes from the real upload which is fine.

EmployeeLayout.js correctly removed docsBypassed filter for 'My Documents'; the comment block at line 59-62 explains the intent clearly — keep it on future refactors so the sidebar entry isn't accidentally re-hidden.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all verified My Documents flows + Verification page render

seed_data_creation: Test-only synthetic Cloudinary URLs posted to pan_card/photo/voter_id for kasper. After tests, pan_card was reset to 'rejected' (with original reason 'Image is blurry. Please re-upload a clearer scan.') to preserve a useful mixed-state for the next iteration. photo and voter_id remain in 'uploaded' state pointing to fake URLs — main agent may want to reset these if a clean state is required, but task notes said terminal state is flexible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kasper's current doc state after this session: aadhaar=verified, pan=rejected (reset), passport/education/experience/offer_letter/relieving_letter=not_uploaded, photo/voter_id=uploaded (synthetic URLs). The /api/onboarding/upload-document endpoint accepts {document_type,file_url,file_public_id,file_name} — does NOT require a real Cloudinary upload for backend coverage. HR notification API key in user.notifications: title, link='/verification', verb 'uploaded' vs 're-uploaded' (controlled by prior status==rejected). Token returned by /api/auth/login lives under key 'token' (not access_token).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All flows behave per spec.
